tldr: MindsDB is an OSS federated data engine for AI-over-data via SQL; Bolt.new is a browser-based AI full-stack app builder. They solve different problems, so migration usually means moving from a data-engine layer to building apps from prompts.
why_switch: You want to ship full web apps, not data pipelines
You prefer an in-browser AI dev environment
Your work is no longer SQL-on-data centric
You want a freemium tier with quick onboarding
You can integrate any data layer from inside Bolt-built apps
what_to_export: MindsDB CREATE DATABASE statements (data source connections)
Backups of any local MindsDB state
export_method: MindsDB is open source and SQL-driven, so most state is reproducible from SQL scripts. Use SHOW DATABASES, SHOW MODELS, and similar statements to dump the definitions of your connected data sources and AI-Tables, then save the resulting CREATE statements as .sql files in version control. Capture the contents of MindsDB's config (config.json or environment variables) without secrets. Note user/role grants for your deployment. If you self-host MindsDB on a database backend, take a database-level backup of its metadata before tearing down the deployment.
import_method: Bolt.new does not import MindsDB definitions because it builds apps, not data engines. Sign in at bolt.new, start a project, and describe the app you want. Use the in-browser editor and live preview to iterate, and deploy from there. If you still need MindsDB-style AI-over-data, integrate MindsDB (or any other database/AI service) from inside the Bolt-built app via standard API calls. Bolt.new is best treated as a starting-from-scratch UI/app layer, with the data engine kept as a separate dependency.
gotchas: Different problem spaces; confirm Bolt.new actually fits your use case.
Bolt.new's repo shows long inactivity in our dataset; do due diligence.
No automated import path from MindsDB to Bolt.new.
You may end up running both products: MindsDB for data, Bolt for UI.
Generated apps may need significant refactor before production use.
MindsDB license on GitHub is non-standard (NOASSERTION); review terms.

email.subject: Re-platforming: MindsDB to Bolt.new for app delivery
email.body:
Hi team,

Over the next 14 days we will pilot Bolt.new for rapid app generation, while reviewing what stays on MindsDB for data-grounded AI. The two tools sit in different layers, so this is a scope realignment, not a swap.

MindsDB stays online during the pilot. Please flag any data workflows you rely on so we can plan transitions or keep them as-is.

Thanks,
Your customer
